text: Hello, this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 你好，这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Phil. And I'm Pippa. Are you a big reader, Phil?
zh: 我是Phil。我是Pippa。Phil，你是个爱读书的人吗？

text: Sometimes yes, sometimes no. Sometimes I go a while without reading anything.
zh: 有时候是，有时候不是。有时候我会有一段时间什么都不读。

text: What about you? Well, I really love to read. I read on the train to work all the time. Ah, well that is a great way to get some reading in, I guess.
zh: 那你呢？我特别喜欢读书。我上班坐火车时一直都在看书。啊，那确实是个抽空阅读的好办法。

text: Yes, and reading, of course, is a life skill, an essential skill which is useful or important in everyone's life.
zh: 是的，阅读当然是一项生活技能，是每个人生活中都有用或重要的基本技能。

text: But reading can be a struggle for people with dyslexia, a learning difficulty making it hard for people to read, write or
zh: 但对患有阅读障碍的人来说，阅读可能很困难，阅读障碍是一种学习困难，会让人难以阅读、书写或

text: spell. It's thought that around one in ten people in the UK are dyslexic,
zh: 拼写。据估计，英国大约每十人中就有一人患有阅读障碍，

text: and many dyslexic children struggle at school where the focus is on reading and writing.
zh: 许多阅读障碍儿童在以读写为重点的学校里学习吃力。

text: But in this episode, we'll be hearing how dyslexics have other skills which allow them to excel in the
zh: 但在这一期节目中，我们将了解阅读障碍者如何拥有其他技能，使他们能够在

text: workplace. As usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and
zh: 职场中表现出色。像往常一样，我们会学习一些实用的新单词和

text: phrases. And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary for this episode on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 短语。记住，你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期的所有词汇。

text: But first, I have a question for you, Phil. Studies have shown that people with dyslexia have enhanced abilities in certain areas
zh: 不过首先，Phil，我有个问题要问你。研究表明，阅读障碍者在某些方面能力更强，

text: and can excel in creative thinking, problem solving and inventiveness.
zh: 能够在创造性思维、解决问题和创新方面表现出色。

text: So, which famous scientist was dyslexic?
zh: 那么，哪位著名科学家患有阅读障碍呢？

text: Was it A. Stephen Hawking, B. Albert Einstein or C. Marie Curie?
zh: 是A.斯蒂芬·霍金，B.阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦，还是C.玛丽·居里？

text: Ooh, I think, I think it's B. I think it's Albert Einstein.
zh: 哦，我觉得，我觉得是B。我觉得是阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦。

text: Well, we'll find out the answer at the end of the programme.
zh: 好的，我们会在节目最后揭晓答案。

text: Now, the British charity Made by Dyslexia shares online learning tools
zh: 英国慈善机构Made by Dyslexia分享在线学习工具，

text: and works with schools and teachers to raise awareness about the condition.
zh: 并与学校和教师合作，提高人们对这种状况的认识。

text: The charity recently collaborated with British celebrities who have dyslexia for this promotional video.
zh: 该机构最近与患有阅读障碍的英国名人合作制作了这段宣传视频。

text: If you're dyslexic, it's kind of your superpower. It's like the way that you think.
zh: 如果你有阅读障碍，这其实是你的超能力。这就是你思考的方式。

text: Our brains, they're wired to, I think, process information differently.
zh: 我们的大脑，我认为，天生就以不同方式处理信息。

text: The way I see the world might be different from somebody else, but that's valid.
zh: 我看世界的方式可能和别人不同，但这同样是有价值的。

text: In fact, it's vital. You might recognise some of those voices,
zh: 事实上，这是至关重要的。你可能认得其中一些声音，

text: including actors Orlando Bloom and Keira Knightley and businessman Richard Branson.
zh: 包括演员奥兰多·布鲁姆和凯拉·奈特莉，以及商人理查德·布兰森。

text: The message is that far from being a disability,
zh: 这个信息是：阅读障碍远非一种残疾，

text: dyslexia is their superpower, the ability to do something other people can't.
zh: 而是他们的超能力——一种别人做不到、他们却能做到的能力。

text: Made by Dyslexia was founded by social entrepreneur Kate Griggs,
zh: Made by Dyslexia由社会企业家凯特·格里格斯创立，

text: and Kate co -authored a report showing that empowering dyslexic workers could boost the global economy by billions.
zh: 凯特共同撰写了一份报告，显示赋能阅读障碍员工可以为全球经济带来数十亿美元的增长。

text: BBC World Service programme People Fixing the World met Kate at the report's London launch event,
zh: BBC世界服务节目《People Fixing the World》在该报告的伦敦发布会上采访了凯特，

text: where she explained why being dyslexic can actually give advantages in life, including some exciting job
zh: 她在那里解释了为什么患有阅读障碍实际上可以带来生活上的优势，包括一些令人兴奋的职业

text: prospects. If you think about what you have to do as a spy, you have to be reading people really quickly,
zh: 前景。想想间谍需要做什么——你必须能非常迅速地读懂人心，

text: spotting complex patterns and solving problems really quickly so you can see whether there's a terror threat.
zh: 发现复杂的模式，快速解决问题，这样才能判断是否存在恐怖威胁。

text: It's natural that dyslexics are brilliant spies.
zh: 阅读障碍者天生就是出色的间谍。

text: Kate thinks dyslexics make good spies,
zh: 凯特认为阅读障碍者能成为出色的间谍，

text: people who secretly collect information about another country's government or organisations.
zh: 也就是秘密收集他国政府或组织信息的人。

text: Why? Because they're good at reading people.
zh: 为什么？因为他们擅长读懂人心。

text: To read people means to be able to understand someone's true intentions
zh: 读懂人心指的是能够通过一个人的肢体语言和行为，而不是他们的言语，来理解其真实意图。

text: through their body language and behaviour, rather than their words.
zh: 通过他们的肢体语言和行为，而不是言语。

text: Also present at the London charity event was space scientist and dyslexic herself, Dr Maggie Adarin -Pocock. Here,
zh: 伦敦慈善活动现场还有一位太空科学家、同时也是阅读障碍者的玛姬·阿达林-波科克博士。

text: Maggie tells BBC World Service's People Fixing the World how being dyslexic contributes different skills and ideas to her project teams.
zh: 在这里，玛姬向BBC世界服务的《People Fixing the World》讲述了阅读障碍如何为她的项目团队带来不同的技能和想法。

text: Well, I think it's recognising people's skills and differences.
zh: 我认为这关乎认识到人们的技能和差异。

text: It's about teamwork and collaboration. We do it in science and that's when we reach for the stars.
zh: 这关乎团队合作与协作。我们在科学领域就是这样做的，这样才能实现远大目标。

text: But to do that we need to know our skills and value all the skills.
zh: 但要做到这一点，我们需要了解自己的技能，并重视所有的技能。

text: Maggie thinks that everyone's skills should be valued or considered important.
zh: 玛姬认为每个人的技能都应该被重视，或者说被认为是重要的。

text: Dyslexics may struggle to read or spell, but their skills in problem solving and communication,
zh: 阅读障碍者可能在阅读或拼写方面有困难，但他们在解决问题和沟通方面的技能，

text: for example, can be essential for scientific success.
zh: 举例来说，对科学上的成功可能是至关重要的。

text: By valuing everyone's contribution, business and science teams can reach for the stars,
zh: 通过重视每个人的贡献，商业和科学团队就能够实现远大目标，

text: an idiom meaning to be ambitious and try to achieve something difficult, even if it seems
zh: reach for the stars是一个习语，意思是有雄心壮志，努力去实现困难的事情，即使看起来

text: impossible. And talking of reaching for the stars,
zh: 不可能。说到实现远大目标，

text: Pippa, I think it's time you revealed the answer to your question about successful scientists.
zh: Pippa，我想是时候公布你之前那个关于成功科学家的问题的答案了。

text: Yes, I asked you which famous scientist was dyslexic.
zh: 是的，我问你哪位著名科学家患有阅读障碍。

text: Was it a Stephen Hawking, b Albert Einstein, or c Marie Curie?
zh: 是A.斯蒂芬·霍金，B.阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦，还是C.玛丽·居里？

text: And you were right, Phil, it was Albert Einstein. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned,
zh: Phil，你答对了，是阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦。好，我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇，

text: starting with life skill – an essential skill such as reading or problem solving, which is useful in everyone's life.
zh: 首先是life skill（生活技能）——像阅读或解决问题这样对每个人生活都有用的基本技能。

text: A superpower is the ability to do something that other people can't.
zh: superpower（超能力）指能够做到别人做不到的事情的能力。

text: A spy is someone who secretly collects information about other countries' governments or organisations.
zh: spy（间谍）指秘密收集他国政府或组织信息的人。

text: If you're good at reading people, you can understand people's real intentions through how they act and behave instead of what they
zh: 如果你擅长read people（读懂人心），你就能通过人们的行为方式而不是言语，理解他们真实的意图。

text: say. If you value something or someone, you consider them to be important.
zh: 如果你value（重视）某事或某人，就是认为它们很重要。

text: And finally, the idiom to reach for the stars
zh: 最后，习语to reach for the stars（摘星揽月/追求远大目标）

text: means to have high or ambitious aims and try to achieve something difficult, even if it seems impossible.
zh: 意思是拥有远大或雄心勃勃的目标，并努力去实现困难的事情，即使看起来不可能。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but remember you can
zh: 我们的六分钟又结束了，不过记住，你可以

text: find many more trending topics plus a quiz and worksheet for this episode on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到更多热门话题，以及本期节目的测验和练习题。

text: Hope to see you there soon, but for now, it's goodbye.
zh: 希望很快能在那里见到你，不过现在，再见啦。

text: from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 来自bbclearningenglish.com
